questions.question:
Consider these two functions and two statements S1 and S2 about them. 

int work1(int *a, int i, int j)
{
    int x = a[i+2];
    a[j] = x+1;
    return a[i+2] - 3;
}
int work2(int *a, int i, int j)
{
    int t1 = i+2;
    int t2 = a[t1];
    a[j] = t2+1;
    return t2 - 3;
}

S1: The transformation form work1 to work2 is valid, i.e., for any program state and input arguments, work2 will compute the same output and have the same effect on program state as work1 

S2: All the transformations applied to work1 to get work2 will always improve the performance (i.e reduce CPU time) of work2 compared to work1



GATE CSE 2006 | Question: 55
options: S1 is false and S2 is false
S1 is false and S2 is true
S1 is true and S2 is false
S1 is true and S2 is true
questions.correct_answer: S1 is false and S2 is false

questions.question:
Consider the following C code segment. 

for (i = 0, i < n; i++)
{ 
    for (j = 0; j < n; j++)
    { 
        if (i%2)
        { 
            x += (4*j + 5*i); 
            y += (7 + 4*j); 
        } 
    } 
}

Which one of the following is false? 



GATE CSE 2006 | Question: 60
options: The code contains loop invariant computation
There is scope of common sub-expression elimination in this code
There is scope of strength reduction in this code
There is scope of dead code elimination in this code
questions.correct_answer: There is scope of dead code elimination in this code

questions.question:
Some code optimizations are carried out on the intermediate code because



GATE CSE 2008 | Question: 12
options: They enhance the portability of the compiler to the target processor
Program analysis is more accurate on intermediate code than on machine code
The information from dataflow analysis cannot otherwise be used for optimization
The information from the front end cannot otherwise be used for optimization
questions.correct_answer: They enhance the portability of the compiler to the target processor

questions.question:
Which one of the following is FALSE?



GATE CSE 2014 Set 1 | Question: 17
options: A basic block is a sequence of instructions where control enters the sequence at the beginning and exits at the end.
Available expression analysis can be used for common subexpression elimination.
Live variable analysis can be used for dead code elimination.
\( x=4*5 \Rightarrow x=20 \) is an example of common subexpression elimination.
questions.correct_answer: \( x=4*5 \Rightarrow x=20 \) is an example of common subexpression elimination.

questions.question:
The minimum number of arithmetic operations required to evaluate the polynomial \( P(X) = X^5+4X^3+6X+5 \) for a given value of \( X \), using only one temporary variable is ______.


GATE CSE 2014 Set 3 | Question: 11

Consider the basic block given below. 

a = b + c 
c = a + d 
d = b + c 
e = d - b 
a = e + b 

The minimum number of nodes and edges present in the DAG representation of the above basic block respectively are



GATE CSE 2014 Set 3 | Question: 34

In the context of abstract-syntax-tree (AST) and control-flow-graph (CFG), which one of the following is TRUE?



GATE CSE 2015 Set 2 | Question: 14
options: In both AST and CFG, let node \( N_2 \) be the successor of node \( N_1 \). In the input program, the code corresponding to \( N_2 \) is present after the code corresponding to \( N_1 \)
For any input program, neither AST nor CFG will contain a cycle
The maximum number of successors of a node  in an AST and a CFG depends on the input program
Each node in AST and CFG corresponds to at most one statement in the input program
questions.correct_answer: The maximum number of successors of a node  in an AST and a CFG depends on the input program

questions.question:
Consider the following \( C \) code segment:

a = b + c;
e = a + 1;
d = b + c;
f = d + 1;
g = e + f;

In a compiler, this code segment is represented internally as a directed acyclic graph \( \text{(DAG)} \). The number of nodes in the \( \text{DAG} \) is _____________



GATE CSE 2021 Set 1 | Question: 50

​​​​​​Consider the following \( \text{ANSI C} \) code segment:

z=x + 3 + y->f1 + y->f2;
for (i = 0; i < 200; i = i + 2) 
{
    if (z > i) 
    {
        p = p + x + 3;
        q = q + y->f1;
    } else 
    {
        p = p + y->f2;
        q = q + x + 3;
    }
}

Assume that the variable \( y \) points to a \( \textsf{struct} \) (allocated on the heap) containing two fields \( \textsf{f1} \) and \( \textsf{f2} \), and the local variables \( \textsf{x, y, z, p, q,} \) and \( \textsf{i} \) are allotted registers. Common sub-expression elimination \( \text{(CSE)} \) optimization is applied on the code. The number of addition and the dereference operations (of the form \( \textsf{ y ->f1} \) or \( \textsf{y ->f2} \)) in the optimized code, respectively, are:



GATE CSE 2021 Set 2 | Question: 30

Which ONE of the following techniques used in compiler code optimization uses live variable analysis?



GATE CSE 2025 | Set 1 | Question: 3
options: Run-time function call management
Register assignment to variables
Strength reduction
Constant folding
